title: Ennealophus foliosus
taxonomy: Ennealophus foliosus is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It is native to South America. The classification of Ennealophus foliosus is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Monocots, order - Asparagales, family - Iridaceae, genus - Ennealophus, species - E. foliosus. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Ennealophus foliosus is a member of the Iridaceae family, which includes other plants commonly known as irises.

intro: Ennealophus foliosus, commonly known as the leafy ennealophus, is a plant species native to the Andes mountain range, belonging to the Campanulaceae family. The leafy ennealophus has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ennealophus foliosus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ennealophus foliosus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
